title: Butea balansae
taxonomy: Butea balansae is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Butea, which is a group of plants commonly known as kesseru or palas. The family Fabaceae, also known as legumes, is a large and diverse group of plants that includes a wide range of species. Butea balansae is native to parts of Asia and is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. This classification helps us understand the plant's relationships and characteristics within the plant kingdom.
uses: Butea balansae has been traditionally used in various folk medicine practices due to its potential medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. In traditional Chinese medicine, Butea balansae is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Research on the plant's medicinal uses is ongoing, and it may offer benefits in the treatment of certain diseases. However, more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.

The roots of the Butea balansae plant are known to contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties may help in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis.
